prompt: Japanese ukiyo-e woodblock print, Edo period (1800s), depicting a lone traveler descending a winding mountain path toward a misty valley below, viewed from a high diagonal vantage point in the style of Hokusai's landscape series. Flat areas of unmodulated color with no digital shading or soft gradients. Bold black ink outlines of even weight. Muted indigo, ochre, and pale grey palette with cream paper ground. Strong diagonal composition with the path leading the eye from upper-right to lower-left valley to represent a cost function. Visible woodblock grain texture in the sky. No photographic depth, no shadows, no 3D rendering, no Western linear perspective. --ar 16:9 --v 6.1 --no shading, 3d, gradients, shadows, text, labels
tradition_approach: I utilized the diagonal 'fukinuki yatai' perspective and flat color planes to map the optimization path. The bold outlines and lack of depth emphasize the mathematical abstraction of a landscape into a topographical cost surface.
